name: TDD-SUBAGENT-BUG-50
title: SKILL.md Step 5 dispatch command shown as bash with subshell expansion that doesn't match Agent invocation
description: rabbit-feature-touch SKILL.md Step 5 (lines 117-126) shows: PROMPT=$(python3 dispatch-tdd-subagent.py ...); Agent(model: opus, prompt: PROMPT). The bash subshell PROMPT=$(...) is shell syntax; Agent(...) is not bash. The example mixes a shell-only construct with a tool call. Real callers must either: (a) write the prompt to a file and pass the path to Agent, or (b) execute dispatch-tdd-subagent.py via Bash and capture stdout into a variable used in a separate Agent call. The example as written is confusing pseudocode. Clarify with separate Bash + Agent tool invocations.
